intro: This is the team's ranked to-do list — the SAME order the board's What's Next shows, highest-value work first. The biggest builds sit at the top; betting work sits at the bottom and is tagged research-only — an experiment we measure, never the main product and never the top of the list.

items.title: Build the Coach Dashboard
items.one_liner: A team-first view: matchup roll-ups, per-player skill breakdowns and player values, all from data we already h

items.title: Build the front-office manager dashboard
items.one_liner: Refresh who is available to play

items.title: Let long board sections start collapsed
items.one_liner: Depth charts and other detail open on a tap, so the board reads at a glance with detail one click away.

items.title: Build one shared player-and-team value backbone
items.one_liner: A single source for player worth and team strength so the coach and manager views can never disagree.

items.title: Smell-test fixes: shooting luck, bench context, thin-sample regression
items.one_liner: Separate real skill from luck and pull tiny samples toward a sensible baseline.

items.title: Turn social-media injury signals into live prediction inputs
items.one_liner: The collector is built; put it on a schedule and wire it into the forecasts.

items.title: Add an official injury feed and learn from past news signals
items.one_liner: So social posts aren't our only source for who is playing tonight.

items.title: Wire the finished foul engine into the live game simulation
items.one_liner: The foul-trouble engine passed its tests but nothing reads it yet; build that connection, then turn it on so f

items.title: Build a usage-and-budget report for the automated work
items.one_liner: See spend at a glance and re-slot the model-building work against it.

items.title: Recover and re-run a request if a session crashes mid-work
items.one_liner: A low-priority safety net so a crash never quietly drops your message.

items.title: Stop cutting off long messages when they are captured
items.one_liner: Raise the capture limit so no request tail is ever lost at intake again.

items.title: Guard against a broken rating build burying a star player
items.one_liner: Add a safety check so an empty rating build can never hide the best players.

items.title: Correct the remaining teams we still rate too high or too low
items.one_liner: Dallas and Las Vegas read too low, Los Angeles too high; extend the shipped fix.

items.title: Make the win-percent and the point-spread tell the same story
items.one_liner: They currently disagree by about a point; reconcile the two cleanly.

items.title: Stop an automatic tune-up from over-correcting the home-court edge
items.one_liner: It is about two games from firming too hard; keep the well-calibrated setting.

items.title: Let batched answers wait for the morning digest without churning
items.one_liner: A non-blocking state so the reminder system does not thrash on a held answer.

items.title: Unify the player-type labels across views
items.one_liner: Two player-style labelings do not line up; create one shared map so the coach views join cleanly.

items.title: Only spin up extra work lanes when the next item clears a value bar
items.one_liner: Stops overnight sessions from over-spending on low-value tail work.

items.title: Reserve the heavy independent double-check for work that ships
items.one_liner: Dead-end results get a light self-check; save the deep check for live changes.

items.title: Always merge onto the latest code, never blind-copy a work branch
items.one_liner: Prevents a stale branch from silently reverting a live feature.

items.title: One-time code-publishing sign-in to keep pushes flowing
items.one_liner: Needs a human sign-in; mostly self-heals now, with a small residual.

items.title: Build a full in-depth tab for the curious reader
items.one_liner: A richer page that sits above betting and below the top-tier surfaces.

items.title: Add freshness badges and a one-line why to each board row
items.one_liner: So every row shows how fresh it is and, in a line, why it matters.

items.title: Build the flagship early-morning deep scouting report
items.one_liner: Full roster projections, opponent scouting, rotation reasoning and fair value.

items.title: Turn the trade-value ranking into a live feature
items.one_liner: Promote the ranked 169-player trade-value list to the manager view and fix its contract-length and pay gaps.

items.title: Ship the season-long player-impact ranking
items.one_liner: A season-long player value view is built and checked; put it live for readers.

items.title: Fix the last stale-data warnings on the board
items.one_liner: Some panels showed hours-old data; add per-panel freshness with a clear flag when a reading is stale.

items.title: Show depth charts and role changes on game cards
items.one_liner: Who is starting and who is stepping up, right on each game card.

items.title: Add a standings and season-outlook tab
items.one_liner: Projected finish and how each team's season could end, graded against the market.

items.title: Grade our season simulation against the futures market
items.one_liner: Title, playoff and win-total odds scored vs the market — the sim is the product.

items.title: Add a we-tested-it-and-it's-noise page
items.one_liner: Publish the ideas we tested and proved dead, to build trust versus tout sites.

items.title: Add descriptive scouting leaderboards
items.one_liner: Built stats like rim protection, clearly labeled scouting rather than predictions.

items.title: Add a head-to-head matchup and player-comparison tool
items.one_liner: Scouting surfaces to compare two teams or two players side by side.

items.title: Bring team pages up to a mainstream sports-site standard
items.one_liner: Fill out the team pages for real product completeness.

items.title: Build the Tempo hub page
items.one_liner: A dedicated team surface and a long-standing target deliverable.

items.title: Show a plain win-percent on each game card
items.one_liner: Turn the margin call into a clear percent chance that each team wins.

items.title: Add player attribute cards and a what-if lineup lab
items.one_liner: Glanceable player identity plus an interactive lineup explorer.

items.title: Add a report-archive browser and disclosure-window tracker
items.one_liner: Browse past reports and track when each call was locked in.

items.title: Add a loud stale badge to the live agent strip
items.one_liner: Mirror the red stale warning and alert if the build process dies.

items.title: Predict realistic return dates for injured players
items.one_liner: Refresh who is available to play

items.title: Measure player impact by court zone
items.one_liner: Break down offense and defense value by area of the floor for sharper scouting.

items.title: Build the next stage of the game simulator
items.one_liner: The player-attribute foundation for a more realistic possession-by-possession simulation.

items.title: Model who guards whom
items.one_liner: Estimate the likely defender on each play to unlock contest, foul and turnover detail.

items.title: Replace hand-set blending numbers with learned ones
items.one_liner: Swap every hand-tuned constant for a weight the data re-learns, wherever it earns its keep.

items.title: Build a fix for each of our biggest error types
items.one_liner: For the ten game-types we predict worst, add the one missing factor that sharpens each.

items.title: Measure which player stats are real skill
items.one_liner: Find how fast each stat stabilizes so we trust the real ones and calm the noisy ones.

items.title: Build player aging curves
items.one_liner: Estimate how players rise and decline with age to sharpen long-range projections.

items.title: Ship the improved minutes-played projections
items.one_liner: Judge minutes on the games that matter most: players stepping into bigger roles.

items.title: Match each prediction range to the game's uncertainty
items.one_liner: Wider ranges for genuinely uncertain games, tighter for clear ones.

items.title: Turn on the roster-news collector
items.one_liner: A built collector sits idle at zero rows; every day off loses free early-news signal.

items.title: Capture every player's birthdate
items.one_liner: Ages power aging curves and career projections; free to grab today, harder to backfill later.

items.title: Capture jersey numbers and listed positions
items.one_liner: Cheap reference data that sharpens roster displays and position priors.

items.title: Capture player headshots
items.one_liner: Portraits make the player pages feel finished; a near-free pull today.

items.title: Refresh the opponent-defense and pace tables
items.one_liner: These stop at last season; rebuild them with this year's games.

items.title: Add college play-by-play with shot locations
items.one_liner: A genuinely new source that unlocks rookie projections we cannot build today.

items.title: Add referee assignments and whistle tendencies
items.one_liner: Bring in who officiates each game to study how crews affect foul rates and totals.

items.title: Turn on the defender-matchup data feed
items.one_liner: The only source we own for who guards whom near the rim; the collector is built and waiting.

items.title: Collect Spanish and French league box scores
items.one_liner: Off-season signal on overseas players, plus an international rookie strength baseline.

items.title: Finish the data-quality guardrails
items.one_liner: Wire the remaining input checks so a bad input is flagged, never silently served.

items.title: Finish the input-quality contracts
items.one_liner: Lock down the last data inputs so a bad date or number cannot slip through unnoticed.

items.title: Collect full 2026 contract terms
items.one_liner: Years, guarantees and options power trade and cap tools; base pay exists but the multi-year terms are missing.

items.title: Send a phone ping when real work ships, and prove it lands
items.one_liner: Hear about finished deliverables automatically, with a daily catch-up for anything big.

items.title: Schedule the prediction-range builder
items.one_liner: It has no schedule and last ran a week ago; wire it so reports stop showing blanks.

items.title: Move leftover notebooks into automatic jobs
items.one_liner: Retire the last manual notebooks to kill stale-password and drift failures.

items.title: Add a weekly where-we-beat-the-market strip
items.one_liner: A short weekly line showing where our calls beat the market that week.

items.title: Test our total-points calls against the market
items.one_liner: Check whether our over/under calls beat the market's total when we disagree.

items.title: Upgrade the accuracy-transparency page
items.one_liner: An honest us-versus-market visual, the credibility centerpiece.

items.title: Point displayed prediction ranges at the better-tuned widths
items.one_liner: So our stated confidence lines up with how often we are actually right.
items.tag: Research only — an experiment, never the top priority

items.title: Rank each night's best bets by confidence
items.one_liner: Combine where we disagree with the market and our track record into a tiered pick list.
items.tag: Research only — an experiment, never the top priority

floor.label: 69 things are lined up in the work queue — a healthy runway (we keep at least 40 ready).
